penalties.description: Video shows extracorporeal suturing (Task 4) rather than peg transfer (Task 1)

frame_analyses.description: Initial view of peg transfer setup with 6 rubber objects on left pegs (3 green, 3 red alternating), empty pegs on right
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard peg transfer configuration, objects properly positioned

frame_analyses.description: Single grasper entering field from right, approaching red object on front peg
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Using only one instrument initially - should use both graspers simultaneously for proper technique

frame_analyses.description: Task appears to have switched to extracorporeal suturing - Penrose drain with suture and marks visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This appears to be Task 4 (Extracorporeal Suture), not Task 1 (Peg Transfer)

frame_analyses.description: Suture being placed through Penrose drain with both needle drivers
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper bilateral instrument use for suturing task

frame_analyses.description: Hands visible forming extracorporeal knot with suture material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper extracorporeal knot tying technique outside the trainer box

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pushed down with knot pusher, loop cinching around drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good knot pusher technique, appropriate tension application

frame_analyses.description: Final positioning of knot on Penrose drain, suture tails visible
External view of hands tying additional throws on suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand positioning for square knot completion

technique_summary: Video demonstrates competent extracorporeal suturing technique but represents wrong task entirely. No peg transfer was performed.
improvement_suggestions: Perform the correct task (peg transfer with rubber objects between pegs)
Use bilateral graspers for object manipulation
Focus on transfer technique rather than suturing skills
